# Carta Pricing Tiers and Feature Set (Official, 2026)

## Summary
Carta's official 2026 pricing page reveals four tiers (Launch, Build, Grow, Scale) with the Launch tier free for companies with up to 25 stakeholders and under $1M raised. 409A valuations only appear in the Grow tier and above, which is a critical gating factor for founders choosing a plan. The Scale tier adds ASC 718/GAAP reporting, IFRS reporting, exit modeling, and IPO advisory. Carta does not publish fixed dollar amounts on its pricing page; pricing is negotiated.

## Key quotations / statistics
- Launch (Free): "Up to 25 stakeholders and $1M raised" - includes cap table management, SAFE modeling, 83(b) forms, ISO management, investor management, equity reporting, HRIS/payroll integration
- Build tier: "Up to 50 stakeholders" - adds white-glove onboarding, priced round modeling, deal closings
- Grow (Recommended): "All Build features plus: board meetings, Form 3921, 409A valuations, and 409A valuation audit support"
- Scale: "All Grow features plus: ASC 718/US GAAP reporting, ASC 718 audit support, Rule 701 management, IFRS financial reporting, exit modeling, IPO advisory"
- Market intelligence: "$3,000-$8,000/year for early-stage (under 50 stakeholders), $10,000-$25,000 for growth-stage (100-300 stakeholders), $30,000-$75,000+ for late-stage" (via Vendr, not Carta official)
- Median Carta contract value: $14,725/year (Vendr data)

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- The 409A valuation gating to the Grow tier is a critical decision point: founders on the free Launch tier must upgrade to get 409A through Carta or use a third-party provider. This informs the platform-selection vs 409A-provider section.
- The 83(b) form generation being available in the free Launch tier is a notable advantage for early-stage founders.
- SAFE modeling is available from Launch tier - important to flag for pre-seed founders.
- Pricing opacity (no public dollar amounts) means the stinger should cite Vendr's market intelligence rather than Carta's own page for cost comparisons.
